rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BAKKIE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 12, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT EATON ELECTRICAL INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Plaintiff shifts the burden, plaintiff creates a triable issue of fact regarding exposure and causation. Based upon the Joel Cohen and Ken Cohen declarations. Motion for summary adjudication infirm. Issues not identified in the Notice of Motion. Also, the only issue this motion presents is exposure/causation. Defendant did not propound any specific interrogatory regarding punitive damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAN MESCHER VS. PACIFIC,GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 12, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT PACIFIC,GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. The common interest privilege applies. (Civil Code section 47(c)). Plaintiff does not create a triable issue of fact regarding malice. "Actual Malice" is established by a showing that the publication was motivated by hatred or ill will toward plaintiff or by a showing that defendant lacked reasonable grounds for belief in the truth of the publication and therefore acted in reckless disregard of plaintiff's rights. (Noel vs. RiverHills Wilsons, Inc. (2003) 113 Cal.App. 4th 1363, 1370.) Here, plainiff shows neither. The Court adopts defendant's proposed order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 12, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT DAVID GILB MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof, and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. The rational basis test applies to this case. There is no evidence of discriminatory intent on the first cause of action. The State had no choice but to provide coverage to non-union employees once they were dropped from participation by the Trust Fund through no fault of the State. The State made coverage available through existing contracts. Plaintiff cites no case law or evidence that creates a triable issue of fact under the factors set forth in Arlington Heights vs. Metropolitan Housing Development Corp. (1977) 429 U.S. 252. Plaintiff's second and third causes of action also fail. Defendant shifts the burden by showing there is no proof of direct and substantial interference with an individual's ability to join a union. Plaintiff fails to present any evidence or case law to create a triable issue of fact that the State's actions directly and substantially interfered with the right to associate. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINISTRATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 12, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is denied. Failure to shift burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMIT MUJUMDAR VS. LAZARD FRERES & CO., LLC A NEW YORK LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 12, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT LAZARD FRERES & CO. LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to first, fourth, sixth, and seventh causes of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second, third, and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff to allege whether employment agreement written, oral or implied by conduct, or attach a copy of the writen agreement. To support estoppel by conduct cause of action, plaintiff must allege that defendant made a representation that plaintiff would receive a bonus while knowing otherwise. =(302/CWW)
